explanation: The vulnerability is confirmed real. In `_updatePoolReward` (lines 837-842), after distributing accumulated rewards via `poolInfo.rewardPerInvestorStored += poolInfo.curReward * endedRounds / poolInfo.participantsCount`, the function never resets `poolInfo.curReward` to zero. A full-text search of the contract confirms `curReward` is only ever incremented (lines 814, 820) or initialized to 0 in the constructor — it is never reset after distribution. This causes every prior round's reward contributions to be redistributed in all subsequent rounds: if R1 was accumulated in round 1 and distributed, it remains in `curReward` and gets added to `rewardPerInvestorStored` again in round 2, round 3, and every round thereafter. Since `claimReward()` mints tokens proportional to accumulated pool rewards, pool participants can claim exponentially inflated token rewards at the protocol's expense. The exploit requires no special privileges — any sequence of deposits across two or more reward rounds (each 24 hours) triggers the compounding. No mitigations exist in the codebase.
